evals.name: full-segment-pitch-utilities-de
evals.prompt: Erstelle einen Segment-Pitch für 'Große Energieversorger & Konzern-Stadtwerke (DE)'. Portfolio: T-Systems. Sprache: Deutsch. Vollständiges Portfolio (kein Lösungsfokus). TIPS-Daten sind im Workspace vorhanden für Utilities/Digitale Transformation.
evals.expected_output: Complete sales-presentation.md + sales-proposal.md with all 4 Corporate Visions arc phases (Why Change, Why Now, Why You, Why Pay). IS/DOES/MEANS Key Differentiators where IS describes T-Systems solutions. 15-25 citations. German throughout including section headers. TIPS trend evidence integrated.
expectations: sales-presentation.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: segment
sales-proposal.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: segment
All 4 arc sections present: Why Change, Why Now, Why You, Why Pay
Key Differentiators table exists with IS/DOES/MEANS columns
Every IS cell describes a T-Systems solution or capability, never the customer's problem
Every DOES cell uses You-Phrasing (Sie/Ihr) with quantified outcomes
Every MEANS cell explains a competitive moat (time, certification, experience barrier)
15-25 numbered citations in Sources section
pitch-log.json shows current_phase done and all 6 phases completed
claims.json has entries with valid source URLs and source_authority scores
Section headers are in German, not English (e.g., no 'The Current Reality', 'Unconsidered Needs')
Why Change section uses PSB structure (Problem, Solution, Benefit)
Why Now section has 2-3 forcing functions with specific dates (not 'bald' or 'demnächst')
Why Pay section stacks 3-4 cost dimensions with a 3-year horizon
TIPS trend evidence appears in Why Change or Why Now findings (signal_origin: tips in research.json)
No methodology jargon in client-facing prose (no 'PSB', 'Corporate Visions', 'Power Position')
German text uses proper umlauts (ä, ö, ü, ß), never ASCII substitutes (ae, oe, ue, ss)
Segment framing uses generic language ('Organisationen in diesem Segment'), not customer-specific

evals.name: focused-ot-security-pitch
evals.prompt: Erstelle einen Segment-Pitch für 'Große Energieversorger & Konzern-Stadtwerke (DE)'. Portfolio: T-Systems. Sprache: Deutsch. Fokus auf Security-Features: endpoint-security, network-security, sase-zero-trust-access, identity-access-management. TIPS-Daten verfügbar.
evals.expected_output: Focused segment pitch where Key Differentiators reference only security/compliance capabilities. Executive hook frames NIS2/KRITIS compliance, not broad digitalization. Why Pay pricing is security-scoped.
expectations: sales-presentation.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter
sales-proposal.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter
All 4 arc sections present
Key Differentiators reference only security-related capabilities (not cloud migration, workplace, etc.)
Every IS cell describes a T-Systems security solution, never the customer's problem
Executive hook focuses on security/compliance theme, not generic digital transformation
Why Now forcing functions include NIS2 and KRITIS-DachG deadlines
Why Pay investment tiers are security-scoped (not full portfolio pricing)
pitch-log.json solution_focus contains the 4 security feature slugs
No methodology jargon in client-facing prose
German section headers and proper umlauts throughout

evals.name: no-tips-graceful-degradation
evals.prompt: Erstelle einen Segment-Pitch für 'Große Energieversorger & Konzern-Stadtwerke (DE)'. Portfolio: T-Systems. Sprache: Deutsch. Vollständiges Portfolio. Keine TIPS-Daten verfügbar.
evals.expected_output: Complete pitch that works without TIPS enrichment. pitch-log.json has tips_path: null. Why Change/Why Now use web research instead of TIPS trend signals. Missing solutions/competitors directories handled gracefully with warnings.
expectations: sales-presentation.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter
sales-proposal.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter
All 4 arc sections present
Every IS cell describes a T-Systems solution, never the customer's problem
pitch-log.json has tips_path: null
Pitch is complete despite no TIPS data (all phases done)
Why Pay section does not hallucinate specific T-Systems pricing tiers if solutions/ directory is empty
Missing solutions or competitors data is acknowledged (warning in pitch-log or narrative note)
15+ citations from web research (compensating for missing TIPS evidence)
No methodology jargon in client-facing prose
German section headers and proper umlauts throughout

evals.name: cogni-works-segment-b2b-sales-dach
evals.prompt: Erstelle einen Segment-Pitch für 'B2B-Vertriebsorganisationen (DACH)'. Portfolio-Pfad: /Users/stephandehaas/GitHub/dev/business-model/cogni-portfolio/cogni-works. Sprache: Deutsch. Vollständiges Portfolio (kein Lösungsfokus). Keine TIPS-Daten verfügbar.
evals.expected_output: Complete sales-presentation.md + sales-proposal.md with all 4 Corporate Visions arc phases. IS/DOES/MEANS Key Differentiators where IS describes cogni-works solutions. 15+ citations. German throughout. Stakeholder review scores in pitch-review.json. No TIPS graceful degradation.
expectations: sales-presentation.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: segment
sales-proposal.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: segment
All 4 arc sections present: Why Change, Why Now, Why You, Why Pay (German headers)
Key Differentiators table exists with IS/DOES/MEANS columns
Every IS cell describes a cogni-works solution or capability, never the customer's problem
Every DOES cell uses You-Phrasing (Sie/Ihr) with quantified outcomes
Every MEANS cell explains a competitive moat (methodology depth, open-source barrier, DACH expertise)
15+ numbered citations in Sources section
pitch-log.json shows current_phase done and all phases completed including review
pitch-log.json has tips_path: null
claims.json has entries with valid source URLs and source_authority scores
Section headers are in German (e.g., 'Warum jetzt handeln', not 'Why Now')
Why Change section uses PSB structure (Problem, Solution, Benefit)
Why Now section has 2-3 forcing functions with specific dates (not 'bald' or 'demnächst')
Why Pay section stacks 3-4 cost dimensions with a 3-year horizon
No methodology jargon in client-facing prose (no 'PSB', 'Corporate Visions', 'Power Position')
German text uses proper umlauts (ä, ö, ü, ß), never ASCII substitutes
Segment framing uses generic language ('Organisationen in diesem Segment'), not customer-specific
pitch-review.json exists with stakeholder scores from three perspectives
pitch-review.json overall_score is 70 or higher after revision passes

evals.name: cogni-works-customer-mindsquare-ag
evals.prompt: Erstelle einen Customer-Pitch für mindsquare AG (Domain: mindsquare.de, Branche: IT-Beratung SAP & Salesforce, ca. 500 Mitarbeiter). Portfolio-Pfad: /Users/stephandehaas/GitHub/dev/business-model/cogni-portfolio/cogni-works. Sprache: Deutsch. Vollständiges Portfolio. Keine TIPS-Daten.
evals.expected_output: Deal-specific presentation + proposal for mindsquare AG. Company-specific research about mindsquare's SAP/Salesforce consulting business. IS/DOES/MEANS adapted to mindsquare's context. Customer-specific framing throughout. Stakeholder review scores.
expectations: sales-presentation.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: customer and customer: mindsquare AG
sales-proposal.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: customer
All 4 arc sections present with German headers
Customer-specific framing: 'mindsquare' referenced in Executive Hook and Why Change sections
Every IS cell describes a cogni-works solution, never the customer's problem
Why Change references mindsquare-specific context (SAP-Ökosystem, Salesforce, Beratungsgeschäft)
Why Now includes timing triggers relevant to IT consulting sector (AI adoption, SAP S/4HANA migration deadlines)
Why Pay scales cost-of-inaction to mindsquare's approximate revenue range
pitch-log.json has pitch_mode: customer and tips_path: null
pitch-log.json has customer_domain: mindsquare.de
15+ citations including mindsquare-specific or SAP/Salesforce ecosystem sources
No methodology jargon in client-facing prose
German section headers and proper umlauts throughout
sales-proposal.md has 'Understanding Your Situation' section (German equivalent) with mindsquare-specific context
pitch-review.json exists with stakeholder scores
Buyer perspective in pitch-review.json adopts VP Sales / Vertriebsleitung role appropriate for IT consulting

evals.name: cogni-works-focused-sales-portfolio-features
evals.prompt: Erstelle einen Segment-Pitch für 'B2B-Vertriebsorganisationen (DACH)'. Portfolio-Pfad: /Users/stephandehaas/GitHub/dev/business-model/cogni-portfolio/cogni-works. Sprache: Deutsch. Fokus auf Features: cogni-sales, cogni-portfolio. Keine TIPS-Daten.
evals.expected_output: Focused segment pitch where Key Differentiators reference only cogni-sales and cogni-portfolio capabilities. Executive hook frames sales methodology and portfolio intelligence, not broad AI consulting. Why Pay pricing scoped to these 2 features.
expectations: sales-presentation.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing pitch_mode: segment
sales-proposal.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter
All 4 arc sections present with German headers
Key Differentiators reference only cogni-sales and cogni-portfolio capabilities (not cogni-trends, cogni-marketing, etc.)
Every IS cell describes a cogni-sales or cogni-portfolio solution, never the customer's problem
Executive hook focuses on sales methodology + portfolio intelligence theme, not generic AI consulting
pitch-log.json solution_focus contains cogni-sales and cogni-portfolio
Why Pay investment tiers are scoped to cogni-sales and cogni-portfolio (not full 12-feature portfolio pricing)
Why Now forcing functions relate to sales/portfolio challenges specifically
15+ citations from web research
No methodology jargon in client-facing prose
German section headers and proper umlauts throughout
pitch-review.json exists with stakeholder scores
pitch-review.json overall_score is 70 or higher after revision passes
